exercises.name: Suspension Push Up
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, ensure your abdominals are engaged throughout. So keep a shorter distance between the bottom of your ribs and the top of your hips.

2.Also, at the bottom of the push up, you’ll want your elbows at about 90 degrees with your hand and elbow in line about mid chest of your body. 

3.With your feet suspended on the straps, begin in the "up" position of a push-up: arms extended, back and neck in a straight line, and core tight. This will be your starting position.

4.Descend into the bottom position of the push-up by flexing at the elbows until they reach a 90-degree position.

5.Repeat for the recommended number of repetitions.

exercises.name: Suspension Reverse Lunge
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, you wanna ensure that at the bottom of the lunges, all joint angles hit approximately 90 degrees.
 
2.It will feel like loading up your back leg most.

3.Initiate the movement by taking a step to the rear, allowing your hips and knees to flex to lower your body. Contacting the back leg through only the ball of the foot, descend until your knee nearly touches the ground. 

4.Use a slow and controlled motion, paying special attention to proper mechanics and posture. The knee should stay in line with the foot, and the thoracic spine should remain neutral.

5.After a brief pause, pull yourself up and return to standing position by driving through the heel of the front leg to extend the knees and hips.

6.When you complete one side, then do the other.

exercises.name: Suspension Reverse Lunge With Hop
exercises.alternateName: TRX Reverse Lunge With Hop jump
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, land softly with bent knees.
 
2.As best you can, make sure your lower body segments hit about 90 degree angles.

3.When you hop up, drive up from your hip as well just your knee and bring the leg in front.

4.Hold on the cable. Initiate the movement by taking a step to the rear, allowing your hips and knees to flex to lower your body. Contacting the back leg through only the ball of the foot, descend until your knee nearly touches the ground. This will be your starting position.

5. Hop by pushing yourself up and raising the leg from the rear to your front while still bent. Then back to the starting position.

6.Once you’ve completed one side, then do the other.

exercises.name: Suspension Side Elbow Plank with Rotation
exercises.alternateName: TRX Side Elbow Plank with Reach
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, lift up with your glutes and abdominals active. Keep a set shoulder by pressing into the mat and engaging your upper torso.

2.With your feet suspended on the straps, get into a side plank position by balancing your weight on one elbow. 

3.Keeping your body straight, lift your hips off the floor. 

4.Extend your free arm all the way up keeping it aligned to your shoulders, arms and elbows straight. This will be your starting position.

5.Then reach as far as you can to the opposite side of the body while slightly twisting upper body. Then back to starting position.

6. When you’re done, repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Suspension Side Full Plank
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, ensure your abdominals are engaged and your glutes are on to maintain a straight line to the side of your body.

2.To help balance through the upper limb, set your shoulder blade in a strong position.

3.With your feet suspended on the straps, get into a side plank position by balancing your weight on one arm.

4.Keeping your body straight, arms and elbows straight, lift your hips off the floor. 

5. When you’re done this side, repeat on the other.

exercises.name: Suspension Side Plank from Elbow
exercises.alternateName: TRX Side Core Plank from Elbow
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, you’ll wanna keep your glutes on and your abdominals. When you’re holding your abdominals, try to keep a tight position between the bottom of your rib cage and top of your hips.

2.You’ll also wanna make sure your shoulder is safe. So, keep the elbow underneath your body and press through it as you loft up your hips. 

3.With your feet suspended on the straps, get into a side plank position by balancing your weight on one elbow. 

4.Keeping your body straight, lift your hips off the floor. 

5.When you’ve completed one side, repeat on the other.

exercises.name: Suspension Single Arm Bicep Curl
exercises.description:
1.For this exercise, to set up the straps, put one handle through the other 1 or 2 times. Or simply grab both handles at the cloth end with one hand.

2.Test to make sure the straps can hold your body weight before starting the exercise.

3.Keep your core engaged, glutes on throughout to maintain neutral spine.

4.Since you’ll be working on your bicep, you wanna keep your elbow high and pull your hand towards your shoulder.

5.To make this exercise easier, move your feet further away from the straps. To make it more challenging, move them closer.

6.When you’ve completed one side, complete the next.

exercises.name: Suspension Single Arm Row
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, you wanna set up the strap so that you put the handles through each other 1 to 2 times.

2.Check to make sure it will hold your body weight.

3.Once you’ve done that, engage your core and glutes throughout to maintain neutral spine.

4.From there, squeeze your back in order to pull yourself up in a row.

5. To make this exercise more challenging, walk your feet towards the strap. To make it easier, walk away.

exercises.name: Suspension Single Leg Squat
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, you can use these straps to gain leverage.

2.At the top of the squat, ensure that your glutes are fully engaged and your core is on so that you get up to neutral spine. 

3.Hold the straps. Raise one foot off the floor. Slightly move your body backwards. 

4.Descend into a squat by flexing your hips and knee. As you squat, extend the non-working leg forward to allow clearance for your movement. 

5. Return to the start by extending through the hips and knee, driving through the heel of your working foot.

6.If you need more assistance to complete this exercise, move your foot further away from the straps. If you wanna make it more challenging, move your foot closer to it.


7.Make sure that once you’re done one side that you do the other side as well.

exercises.name: Suspension Single Leg Squat Jump
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, you can use these straps for leverage.

2.After hopping, ensure that you land on a soft leg rather than locking out your leg when you land. 

3.Hold the straps. Raise one foot off the floor. Slightly move your body backwards. 

4.Descend into a squat by flexing your hips and knee. As you squat, extend the non-working leg forward to allow clearance for your movement. 

5. Push yourself upward then hop while keeping the leg raised and land softly then back to a squat position.

6.To make this exercise easier, you can move your foot in landing spot further away from the straps. To make it more challenging, keep your foot in landing place closer. 


7.Once you’ve completed one side, do the other.

exercises.name: Suspension Split Rear Fly
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, keep your core engaged and glutes on maintaining a straight line throughout your body during the entire exercise. 

2.Check to make sure it will hold your body weight.

3.Pull yourself up with your one arm all the way up and the other arm going down.

4.To make this exercise easier, walk yourself away from the straps. To make it more challenging, walk towards.

exercises.name: Suspension Split Squat
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, maintain your tight core and neutral spine.

2.Also, don’t let this knee pass over the top of your toes on the standing side.

3.And on the suspended side, load up that back leg as much as you can with your body weight.

4.Lower the suspended foot all the way to back as far as you can until your knee nearly touches the ground. Then push yourself keeping a straight body.

5.Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

6.Once you’re done one side, repeat on the other.

exercises.name: Suspension Split Squat with Hop
exercises.alternateName: TRX bulgarian lunge with plyo jump
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, ensure that you land on a soft knee. Keep your core engaged in neutral spine

2.Don’t let the knee on the front-loaded side pass over top of your toes.

3.On the suspended side, load up as much body weight as you can.

4.Lower the suspended foot all the way to back as far as you can until your knee nearly touches the ground. Then hop by push yourself keeping a straight body.

5.Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions. 

6.When you complete one side, repeat on the other.

exercises.name: Suspension Squat
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, you wanna make sure that you have a safe back.

2.To do so, you wanna set it with slight arch at the bottom of the squat. 

3.Also, since this is a lower body exercise, you wanna ensure the you’re driving from your hips so your glutes will be active at the top of the squat.

4.With your back, shoulders, and core tight, push your knees and butt out and you begin your descent.  

5.To make this exercise more challenging, move your feet closer to the straps. To make it easier, move your feet further away.

exercises.name: Suspension Standing Figure Four Stretch
exercises.description:
1.In this stretch, you’ll be crossing your leg and stretching out the hip on the side that you lift.

2.Use the straps for balance as you sit back.

3.Hold for 20 – 30 seconds then repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Suspension Standing Roll Out
exercises.alternateName: TRX Standing Roll Out lat pull over
exercises.description:
1.While performing this exercise, maintain a neutral and tight position between the upper part of your pelvis and the bottom of your rib cage. That will ensure your abdominals are on and active.

2.You’ll also wanna keep a straight line throughout your body.

3.Keep your arms straight throughout the exercise.

4.Grab the handles with your palms facing the floor. Stand closer to the straps with your back towards the suspension and your heels up. This will be your starting position.

5.To begin, extend your body forward. As you decline, flex the shoulder to move the straps above your head. Ensure that your arms remain extended throughout the movement.

6.To make this exercise more challenging, add length to the straps and have a more horizontal position to your body. To make it easier, shorten the straps and be more upright.

exercises.name: Suspension Straight Leg Raise
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, ensure your abdominals are engaged and your lower back is pulled in neutral position.

2.This will be particularly challenging as your legs get closer to the ground.

3.To ensure they are strong enough and active, press on to the handles and engage your upper body to help the lower body.

4.Lie with your back flat on the ground, hold the handles with your arms straight and hands above your torso. This will be your starting position.

5.As you keep your legs extended, straight as possible with your knees slightly bent but locked raise your legs until they make a 90-degree angle with the floor. Exhale as you perform this portion of the movement and hold the contraction at the top for a second.

6.As you inhale, slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.

exercises.name: Suspension T Delt Fly
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, keep your core and glutes engaged to keep neutral spine. 

2.At the top of the fly, your arm and hand will be in this position.

3.Grasp the handles with both hands with your palms facing each other.

4.With your arms extended, lean back and adjust your relative position to change the degree of difficulty. 

5.With your body straight and your arms extended, you will be in the starting position.

6.Abduct the shoulder while keeping the elbow slightly bent to pull yourself up to perform a reverse fly motion.

7.To make this exercise easier, walk your feet back. To make it more challenging, move your feet forward.

exercises.name: Suspension Tricep Extension
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, you wanna maintain a straight line from your toes to your elbow overhead.

2.Squeeze your biceps into the side of your head and keep both arms and elbows pointing to the same direction as you extend.

3.Grab the handles with both arms raised and your back towards the suspension. This will be your starting position.

4.Extend your body forward as far as you can while keeping your biceps to the side of your head and elbows bent. Then back to starting position.

5.To make this exercise more challenging, lengthen the straps and have a greater decline to your body. To make is easier, shorten the straps and be more upright.

exercises.name: Suspension Tricep Kickback
exercises.description:
1.For this exercise, keep your core tight and glutes on to maintain neutral spine.

2.You’re gonna be working your triceps so isolate them by keeping your elbows tucked in to the side of your body.

3.Let yourself fallback then pull yourself while keeping your elbows tucked to the side. Then, back to starting position.

4.To make this exercise easier, move your feet further away from the straps. To make it more challenging, move them closer.

exercises.name: Suspension Wide Mountain Climber
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, keep your abdominals engaged. You’ll be bringing the knee on the one side around wide to the elbow on the same side of your body. 

2.With your feet suspended on the straps, get into starting position by balancing your weight on your hands, arms and elbows straight. 

3.Keeping your body straight, lift your hips off the floor.

4.Start with one leg, bring the knee to the side around wide to the elbow on the same side of the body.  Then back to starting position. Proceed with the other leg.

exercises.name: Suspension Wide Stance Hip Hinge
exercises.alternateName: TRX Wide Stance Hip Hinge Hamstring stretch
exercises.description:
1.During the stretch, you could feel it in any number of places depending on your flexibility. You might feel it in your chest, front or your shoulder, inside of your legs or even your hamstrings at the back of the legs.

2.Make sure you get a sufficiently wide stance in order to feel a little bit of stretch on the inside of the legs before you rock back. 

3.Begin exercise with both hands holding the straps with arms straight, and your feet apart creating a wide stance.

4.Pull yourself backwards letting your lower back form a slight curvature then moving your feet further away from each other to adjust the stretch.

5.Hold for approximately 20 – 30 seconds.

exercises.name: Suspension Y Delt Fly
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, keep your core and glutes engaged to maintain neutral spine. 

2.At the top of the fly, your arms will be in this position, forming the letter Y.

3.Grasp the handles with both hands with your palms facing each other.

4.With your arms extended, lean back and adjust your relative position to change the degree of difficulty. 

5.With your body straight and your arms extended, you will be in the starting position.

6.Abduct the shoulder while keeping the elbow slightly bent to pull yourself up to perform a reverse fly motion.

7.To make this exercise easier, walk your feet back. To make it more challenging, move your feet forward.

exercises.name: Swimmers
exercises.description:
1. Start by lying on your chest on the mat, with arms and legs fully extended. 

2. Raise one leg upwards, while raising the opposite hand upwards.

3. Return to the start position, then do the same on the other side.

exercises.name: T Push Up
exercises.description:
1. When performing this stretch, you'll want to spring up out of the loaded push up position and allow your body to open and rotate. To help facilitate that, you'll want to allow your feet to roll over to the side.

2. Lie on the floor face down and your feet shoulder width apart. This is your starting position.

3. Lower yourself downward until your chest almost touches the floor as you inhale.

4. As you push your body up, raise one arm up while rotating your body to the side and your feet to roll over to the side.

5. Then back to starting position.

exercises.name: Table Top Full Arm Thoracic Rotation
exercises.description:
Begin in a tabletop position. Place one hand behind your head. 

Rotate your torso to bring the elbow toward the opposite hand, then rotate upward, lifting the elbow toward the ceiling. Switch sides.

exercises.name: Table Top Half Arm Thoracic Rotation
exercises.description:
1. Start on your hands and knees on the mat. 

2. Drop down to your elbows while keeping your hips directly over your knees. 

3. Place one hand behind your head, and reach the elbow of that hand towards the ceiling by rotating through your torso. 

4. Rotate completely the other way, reaching the elbow towards the mat. 

5. Repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Table top heel raises with knee add
exercises.description:
If you do not have a block use a ball
Or towel between knees and squeeze

exercises.name: Tall Stand to Squat Jump
exercises.description: Start in a tall standing posture, drop into a squat, then jump explosively.

exercises.name: T-Bar Row
exercises.description: Stand over the T-bar, hinge at hips, and row the bar toward your torso. Squeeze shoulder blades together, then lower.

exercises.name: The PERFECT Barbell Row (5 Steps)
exercises.description: The PERFECT Barbell Row (5 Steps)

exercises.name: The Right Way to Smith Squat for MAXIMUM Quad Growth
exercises.description: The Right Way to Smith Squat for MAXIMUM Quad Growth

exercises.name: Thread the Needle Alternating Back Stretch
exercises.alternateName: Eye of the Needle Parsva Balasana Variant
exercises.description:
1. For this exercise, try to keep your body as quiet as possible, that means not moving your hips around while performing this movement.

2. Position yourself on your hands and knees on the ground. This will be your starting position.

3. Reach left arm all the way to the right as far as possible. Then back to starting position. If you have the rotation, you may lay your shoulder on the ground and lean back for a deeper stretch.

4. Repeat the movement.

exercises.name: Thread the Needle Glute Stretch
exercises.description: Lie on your back, cross one ankle over the opposite thigh. Pull the uncrossed leg toward your chest to stretch the glute. Hold, then switch sides.

exercises.name: Toe Crunch
exercises.description:
1) Lie in your back and as best as you can, straighten your legs up into the air. You might need to bend your knees if your hamstrings are too tight. 

2) From here, roll your rib cage up and reach for your toes and then back down. 

3) If it’s too easy, try reaching farther into the bottom of your feet.

exercises.name: Toe Tap
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, keep your weight on the outside foot and minimize the weight on the toe on the ball. 

2) Hop and switch.
